title: praxelis clematidea
intro: Praxelis clematidea, also known as Whitebrush, is a shrub native to the Americas and is a member of the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing relief from gastrointestinal problems. Herbal preparations of Praxelis clematidea can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
